questions.q: Who is considered the founder of the 'Varna' system?
questions.explanation: Manu is considered the founder of the 'Varna' system, as mentioned in Manusmriti. Others are associated with other aspects of Indian society.
questions.topic: Origin and Development of Caste System

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the process of assigning status to individuals within a society?
questions.explanation: The term 'stratification' is used to describe the process of assigning status to individuals within a society based on their social position. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: What is the main difference between 'Jati' and 'Varna'?
opts: (A) Jati is a more rigid system than Varna
(B) Varna is a more rigid system than Jati
(C) Jati is a local system and Varna is a pan-Indian system
(D) Varna is a system of occupations and Jati is a system of territorial organisation
questions.explanation: Jati is a local system and Varna is a pan-Indian system. Jati is more rigid than Varna.

questions.q: Who wrote the text 'The Laws of Manu'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Laws of Manu' is attributed to Manu, who is considered the founder of the 'Varna' system.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the practice of marrying within one's own social group?
questions.explanation: The term 'endogamy' is used to describe the practice of marrying within one's own social group. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: Who is the author of the text 'The Institutes of Hindu Law'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Institutes of Hindu Law' is attributed to Manu, who is considered the founder of the 'Varna' system.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the practice of marrying outside one's own social group?
questions.explanation: The term 'exogamy' is used to describe the practice of marrying outside one's own social group. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: What is the main difference between 'Class' and 'Caste'?
opts: (A) Class is a more rigid system than Caste
(B) Caste is a more rigid system than Class
(C) Class is a system of economic stratification and Caste is a system of social stratification
(D) Class is a pan-Indian system and Caste is a local system
questions.explanation: Class is a system of economic stratification and Caste is a system of social stratification.

questions.q: Who is the author of the text 'The Institutes of Narada'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Institutes of Narada' is attributed to Narada, who is associated with the 'Varna' system.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a higher social group?
questions.explanation: The term 'hypergamy' is used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a higher social group. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: Who is the author of the text 'The Institutes of Bhrigu'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Institutes of Bhrigu' is attributed to Bhrigu, who is associated with the 'Varna' system.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a lower social group?
questions.explanation: The term 'hypogamy' is used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a lower social group. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: What is the main difference between 'Class' and 'Caste' in terms of their origins?
opts: (C) Class and Caste have the same origin
(D) Class originated in a different region than Caste
questions.explanation: Caste originated before Class, with the 'Varna' system being the earliest known system of social stratification.

questions.q: Who is the author of the text 'The Institutes of Yajnavalkya'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Institutes of Yajnavalkya' is attributed to Yajnavalkya, who is associated with the 'Varna' system.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a lower social group, but with the consent of the higher social group?
questions.explanation: The term 'hypogamy with consent' is used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a lower social group, but with the consent of the higher social group. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: What is the main difference between 'Jati' and 'Varna' in terms of their social organization?
opts: (A) Jati is a more complex system than Varna
(B) Varna is a more complex system than Jati
(C) Jati is a local system and Varna is a pan-Indian system
(D) Jati is a system of territorial organization and Varna is a system of occupational organization
questions.explanation: Jati is a system of territorial organization and Varna is a system of occupational organization.

questions.q: Who is the author of the text 'The Institutes of Apastamba'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Institutes of Apastamba' is attributed to Apastamba, who is associated with the 'Varna' system.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a higher social group, but without the consent of the higher social group?
questions.explanation: The term 'hypergamy without consent' is used to describe the practice of marrying a person from a higher social group, but without the consent of the higher social group. Others are related concepts.

questions.q: What is the main difference between 'Class' and 'Caste' in terms of their relationship with the economy?
opts: (A) Class is more closely tied to the economy than Caste
(B) Caste is more closely tied to the economy than Class
(C) Class and Caste have the same relationship with the economy
(D) Class is not tied to the economy and Caste is tied to the economy
questions.explanation: Class is more closely tied to the economy than Caste, with Class being a system of economic stratification.

questions.q: Who is the author of the text 'The Institutes of Katyayana'?
questions.explanation: The text 'The Institutes of Katyayana' is attributed to Katyayana, who is associated with the 'Varna' system.
